description_long: This object is a large, historical-style cannon mounted on a wooden carriage with four wheels. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and tapers slightly towards the muzzle, with a flared end and a prominent touch hole at the rear. The carriage is constructed from thick wooden planks, reinforced with metal bands and bolts, and features two large, solid wheels on each side for mobility. The overall finish of the cannon is dark, with the metal barrel appearing black and the wooden carriage a muted brown. The wheels are robust and designed to support the heavy weight of the cannon, while the carriage includes metal hardware for structural integrity. This type of object would typically be found in historical or military-themed urban settings, such as parks, museums, or as a decorative piece in public squares.
description: A large, black metal cannon mounted on a sturdy wooden carriage with four wheels and metal reinforcements. The object has a historical appearance, with a long barrel and robust construction.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the cannon barrel and the wooden carriage with two large wheels and metal reinforcements.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular muzzle of the cannon and the symmetrical arrangement of the wheels and carriage.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the length of the barrel and carriage details from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the breech of the cannon and the back of the carriage, with the wheels and metal hardware visible.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, long cylindrical barrel with flared muzzle and no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_action_mechanism: none visible (cannon, not a shotgun action mechanism)
